steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the circle facts first: radii are equal, a diameter sets up a right angle, a tangent is perpendicular to the radius, and central/inscribed angles connect to arcs.

steps.title: Use the unit circle period
steps.content: Sine repeats every $2\pi$. Since $42\pi=21(2\pi)$, the angle makes exactly $21$ full rotations around the unit circle.

steps.title: Return to the starting point
steps.content: After a whole number of full rotations, the terminal point is back at $(1,0)$. Sine is the y-coordinate of the terminal point.
